text: Hey team, we need to finalize the cloud vendor selection. We've been evaluating AWS, Azure, and GCP for the past month.

I've completed the technical evaluation. AWS has the best container orchestration with EKS, and their ML services like SageMaker are very mature. Azure is close but their pricing model is less transparent.
From a cost perspective, I've run the numbers. AWS would cost us approximately $45K/month, Azure $52K/month, and GCP $48K/month for our projected workload.
Security-wise, all three meet our compliance requirements. However, AWS has the most mature IAM system and better audit logging capabilities.
What about vendor lock-in risk? That's been a concern from leadership.

If we use Kubernetes and containerize everything, vendor lock-in is minimal. We should avoid proprietary services where possible. AWS has the largest ecosystem of third-party tools that help with portability.
Also worth noting - 3 of our 5 engineers already have AWS certifications. Training costs for Azure or GCP would be significant, estimated around $15K for the team.

Good points all around. Let me summarize: AWS wins on cost ($45K vs $52K Azure), technical maturity (EKS, SageMaker), security (IAM, audit logs), and team expertise. The team has decided to go with AWS as our primary cloud provider, with a multi-cloud strategy for disaster recovery using GCP. This was chosen over Azure due to higher cost and over exclusive GCP because of our team's existing AWS expertise.
Agreed. The key reasons are cost-effectiveness, better container support, and reduced training overhead since our team already knows AWS well.
I'll start setting up the AWS infrastructure this week. We should have the staging environment ready by next Friday. I'll also document our Kubernetes setup for portability.
